intro.title: Hi, I'm Yuan Zehao
intro.desc:
MS ECE @ UCLA • Robotics & Hardware Engineer
Focus: Mechanical design, embedded/control, and robotic systems integration.
Email: YOUR_EMAIL

item1.title: RoboMaster: Hero Robot System
item1.desc: Led/owned key mechanical subsystems and integration for a RoboMaster Hero-class robot. Worked across design, manufacturing, assembly, and iteration to deliver competition-ready performance under tight schedules.

item2.title: Humanoid / Mobile Manipulation Hardware
item2.desc: Designed and iterated on humanoid / mobile manipulation hardware components (structures, mounting, packaging). Emphasized DFM/DFA, maintainability, and robustness for real-world testing.

item3.title: Mixed-Signal & Power Projects
item3.desc: Course/project work across mixed-signal IC and power electronics (e.g., PLL modeling/design, DC-DC system-level analysis). Focused on engineering rigor: models, trade-offs, and validation.

project1.title: RoboMaster: Hero Robot System
project1.desc:
A competition-grade robot platform emphasizing reliability, serviceability, and fast iteration.

My contributions centered on mechanical subsystem design, integration, and manufacturability. I worked closely with cross-functional teammates to ensure mechanisms, wiring/packaging, and assembly workflows supported rapid debugging and match-day maintenance.

project2.title: Humanoid / Mobile Manipulation Hardware
project2.desc:
Hardware development for humanoid/mobile manipulation platforms.

Work included structural design, packaging, and iteration based on test feedback. Key priorities: robustness, maintainability, and efficient assembly (DFM/DFA).

project3.title: Mixed-Signal & Power Projects
project3.desc:
A collection of mixed-signal IC and power-electronics related projects.

Typical workflow: define requirements → build analytical/simulation models → explore trade-offs → validate with results and documentation.
